extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won Stage 8 sprint to Limoges with a long-range effort after STUYVEN Jasper's leadout, holding off PHILIPSEN Jasper on the uphill finish.
tactical_patterns: PEDERSEN launched from extreme distance before 1km on rising finish, gambling power over positioning—worked despite fading visibly.
Trek-Segafredo isolated PEDERSEN second-wheel behind STUYVEN through final corners, prioritizing clean line over traditional last-100m launch.
YATES Simon lost 47 seconds in late-stage crash, dropping from GC page one—positioning risk persists even on flat stages.

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: demonstrated ability to execute ultra-long sprint on uphill finish, confirming power-over-position strategy viable on gradient finales
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: leadout strength extends beyond 300m, can deliver through two corners at sustained threshold before 1km marker
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: closed hard but second-place finish suggests positioning disadvantage or insufficient power reserve for gradient finish
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: lost 47 seconds in crash during routine flat stage, revealing positioning vulnerability even outside GC-critical stages
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: retained entire team through flat sprint stage, showing Visma tactical discipline in low-priority stage type
